metadata.business_context: 3 SOMS AI Wine Advisor Knowledge Base
metadata.note: These are the reference point wines that define the 3 SOMS thesis: single-vineyard, old vines, boutique scale, undervalued
metadata.user_tasting_status: All wines personally tasted by founder with master sommeliers in Greece (2023-2024)

wineries.name: Artemis Karamolegos
wineries.description: One of the most dynamic and rapidly evolving wineries on Santorini. Third-largest winery by volume on the island, but quality-focused with single-vineyard expressions from 150+ year old vines. Founded by Artemis Karamolegos, inspired by his grandfather who started making wine in the 1950s.
wineries.philosophy: Terroir-driven winemaking focused on expressing the diverse character of Assyrtiko across Santorini's unique volcanic parcels. Minimal intervention, single-vineyard focus, old vine preservation.

vineyard_info.soil_description: Pumice-rich volcanic ash, basalt, granite, obsidian. High silica oxides and metals create acidic/low pH soils that amplify mineral/marine/saline character.
vineyard_info.unique_terroir_note: Santorini's volcanic explosion in 1613 BCE created hostile soils immune to Phylloxera—vines are ungrafted, some over 150 years old. Roots can exceed 20 meters seeking water.

production_style.approach: Old-world, single-vineyard focus with modern precision
production_style.vineyard_training: Traditional kouloura (basket) training to protect from wind and retain moisture
production_style.innovations: First orange wine on Santorini, experimenting with custom cement tanks

wines.name: Papas

tasting_profile.development_notes: Develops petrol complexity with bottle age (5+ years). Give 30 minutes to breathe before enjoying.

wines.tasting_notes_detailed: Santorini's most sophisticated expression. Complex nose with citrus, peach, pear, herbs (chamomile, thyme), nuts, honey, and intense minerals. Salty, mineral, wonderfully smoky like gunpowder on the palate. Sharp acidity with nerve and an aftertaste that lasts 'forever'. One of the wines that can evolve for at least a decade. Captures the essence of 150-year-old vines on volcanic terroir.

critic_scores.jancis_robinson: Positively vibrating with energy. 1350 bottles made.
critic_scores.wineanorak: 94/100 - Powerful and striking, lashed with salinity

user_experience.reaction: The best white wine I had tried up to that point. Notes of petrol, citrus.
user_experience.significance: The wine that introduced me to the idea of single vineyards. This defined the category.
user_experience.insight: Assyrtiko can feel harsh to newcomers—harsh in the sense of a natural wine. The single vineyards are a great entry point for their smoothness, richness and delicacy.
user_experience.thesis_fit: Peak expression of the 3 SOMS thesis: 150-year vines, single vineyard, volcanic terroir, €100 price point vs €150-240 for equivalent Puligny-Montrachet Premier Cru.

critic_scores.wineanorak: 94/100 - Striking and vibrant, positively ringing with flinty minerality

price.note: More accessible entry to serious Karamolegos Assyrtiko

user_other_tastings.ftelos: Tried. Another excellent single vineyard expression.
user_other_tastings.34: The Aidani and Athiri add fruitiness that tames the strong character of Assyrtiko. Great for those finding pure Assyrtiko too intense.
user_other_tastings.northern_greece_malagousia: Tried a fantastic Malagousia blend from their winery in the north of Greece.

wineries.name: Domaine Sigalas
wineries.description: Pioneering excellence in Santorini winemaking. Founded by Paris Sigalas, a mathematics professor who professionalized the family winery. First to produce single-vineyard Assyrtiko (Kavalieros, 2011). Known for meticulous vineyard work, profound respect for old vines, and uncompromising pursuit of terroir expression.
wineries.philosophy: Never content with preserving heritage—pushing boundaries through meticulous vineyard work and terroir expression. Single-vineyard, minimal intervention, old vine focus.

recent_news.event: Acquired by Kir-Yianni, uniting three of Greece's most prestigious wine regions

awards: Robert Parker Wine Advocate: 93 points (Santorini Barrel 2019)

vineyard_info.kavalieros_description: Overlooks the dramatic Santorini caldera from one of the highest altitudes on the island. One of the best sub-appellations.

wines.name: Kavalieros
wines.vineyard_note: Highest altitude vineyard site on Santorini. Name means 'on top'—winery believes this is one of the best vineyards in one of the best sub-appellations.
wines.historic_significance: First single-vineyard Assyrtiko ever produced in Santorini

tasting_profile.finish: Unique lingering persistency, gripping, doesn't let go

wines.tasting_notes_detailed: Made from 100% Assyrtiko at Santorini's highest vineyard site. Delivers sharp, vertical aromas. The 18-month lees aging creates volume and creaminess while the characteristic salinity makes it irresistible. Concentrated and powerful yet gorgeously structured—never seeming overripe. Elegant in mid-palate but ever more powerful on finish. Simply gripping, fresh, lively, beautifully constructed.

critic_scores.decanter_2024: 92 points (Silver) - Salted quince, green olive skin, mineral nuances. Lovely texture, refreshing.
critic_scores.robert_parker_2018: Textured, ripe and gorgeously structured. Long finish, full-bodied.
critic_scores.wine_advocate_2017: 93 points - Most elegant of the Sigalas Assyrtikos. Impeccably balanced.

aging_potential.note: Needs cellar time to become more expressive

user_experience.reaction: 💯 Exquisite. 'This is Assyrtiko single-vineyard. This is my category.'
user_experience.tasting_notes: Similar tasting notes to Karamolegos Papas.
user_experience.comparison_to_papas: Slightly cheaper and more accessible in my opinion, without sacrificing on taste. Similar terroir expression, younger vintage so no petrol notes yet.
user_experience.thesis_fit: Confirms the category: single-vineyard, high-altitude, old vines, €100 price point.

wineries.name: The Knack Project
wineries.description: Small, independent natural winery founded by Christos Koulouriotis. Born from passion—a wine lover who got his DipWSET (Level 4), then decided to make wine. 'I started backwards; I did not have the vines to make wine. First I learned to taste, then I decided to make my wine.' Total production just 5,000 bottles/year.
wineries.philosophy: Low-intervention, authentic, unfiltered wines with true personality. Organic farming, spontaneous fermentation, minimal interference. Bold character, pure taste, fresh voice in new generation of Greek wine.

founder.name: Christos Koulouriotis
founder.background: Software professional who studied DipWSET, then built a winery. No prior viticulture experience—just deep love for wine and an inquiring mind.
founder.approach: Learned to taste first, then decided to make wine. Working with old vines he found 'by luck instead of personal choice.'

tasting_profile.character: Natural expression of Mouhtaro—authentic, unfiltered, with true personality

user_experience.wines_tasted: Two Mouhtaros—one barrel aged, the other not.
user_experience.barrel_aged_notes: One of the most interesting reds I've tried in a while. It tasted like nectar, with strawberry notes. Reminded me of Sicilian reds without the acidity.
user_experience.unoaked_notes: Softer on the palate with similar berry notes.
user_experience.thesis_fit: Micro-production (5,000 bottles total across all wines), natural winemaking, rare indigenous grape. The opposite of commercial—pure passion project.

wineries.name: Samartzis Estate
wineries.sub_region: Askri, Valley of the Muses, Boeotia (near Thebes)
wineries.description: Family-run winery rooted in tradition yet driven by innovation. Located in the Valley of the Muses, about 1.5 hours northwest of Athens. Known for reviving the nearly extinct Mouhtaro grape. Embraces a 'one vineyard, one wine' philosophy.
wineries.philosophy: Expressing terroir and grape identity. Reviving indigenous varietals. Sustainable farming, hand harvest, every plot has its own rhythm. Wines that wear their origin like a badge—unmistakably Askri, unpolished, and true.

founders.panos_samartzis: Former pharmacist turned vintner. The visionary behind Mouhtaro's revival.

vineyard_info.climate: Cool mountain breezes from Mount Helicon, abundant Boeotian sunshine

unique_story.mouhtaro_revival: Mouhtaro is a rare, late-ripening local red grape that almost died out in the 1970s. Panos Samartzis kept it alive through persistence, rescuing it from the brink of extinction. Now produces single-varietal expressions and blends.
unique_story.valley_of_muses: Vineyards on the southeastern slopes of Mount Helicon, mythological home of the Muses. Connection to Greek cultural heritage.

wines.name: Mouhtaro Papanikolas
wines.vineyard_location: Southern slopes of Mount Helicon, Valley of the Muses
wines.grape_story: Mouhtaro is an old, late-ripening red grape rescued from extinction. This is the 100% single-varietal expression—the purest form.
wines.tasting_notes_detailed: The purest expression of the rescued Mouhtaro grape. Deep black ruby color with complex aromatics of black forest fruits, violet, chocolate, tobacco, and sweet spice. Rich, creamy, layered palate with refreshing acidity that cuts through the density. Full body with very long aftertaste. A wine that showcases what was almost lost forever.

user_experience.reaction: Quite candidly, it's been a while—but the savory, herbaceous, tomato-leaf character stays with you.
user_experience.significance: Revival of an almost-extinct grape. Represents discovery and preservation.
user_experience.thesis_fit: Unique varietal nobody outside Greece knows. Boutique production. Story of rescue and revival. This is what 3 SOMS is about—finding wines the world hasn't discovered.

wines.name: M (Merlot-Mouhtaro)
wines.tasting_notes_detailed: Although Merlot-weighted, the local Mouhtaro makes itself felt in the deliciously different flavor. Red cherry and pomegranate with tomato leaf, black pepper, and gentle fine-sand tannins. Full and rich with vanilla, chocolate and spice, followed by soft tannins and refreshing finish.

critic_notes.joanna_simon: Deliciously different flavour—red cherry, pomegranate, tomato-leaf, black pepper, fine-sand tannins.
critic_notes.jancis_robinson: Transparent pillar-box red. Luminous, accessible, sautéed cranberries. More character than 100% Merlot.

price.note: Excellent value entry point to Mouhtaro character

wineries.name: Bizios Estate
wineries.description: Family-run organic estate at the highest point of PDO Nemea. Founded by Elias Bizios, second-generation winemaker born and raised in Asprokampos village at 900m altitude. His love for the earth and vine made winemaking his vocation. Built his winery at the edge of his vineyard to express the everyday life of the grape.
wineries.philosophy: Organic viticulture (certified nearly 30 years), dry-farmed, low yields for maximum concentration. Absolute respect for environment. Genuine expression of Asprokampos terroir—blending old with new, keeping tradition alive.

founder.name: Elias Bizios
founder.background: Son of a viticulturist, born and raised in Asprokampos
founder.beloved_variety: Agiorgitiko—dark crimson like the blood of Hercules, who legend says wandered this area to kill the lion of Nemea

vineyard_info.altitude_significance: Highest point of PDO Nemea. Cool night breezes from Mount Ziria benefit grape growing.
vineyard_info.location: Asprokampos village, in natural bowl between mountains of Ziria and Gavria
vineyard_info.soil_description: Limestone bedrock with clay topsoil, excellent drainage

production_style.approach: Organic, dry-farmed, old-world with modern precision
production_style.facility: Modern winery with 200-tonne capacity, stainless steel tanks, oak barrel cellar

wines.name: Nemea PDO Agiorgitiko

tasting_profile.acidity: Lively, remarkable freshness for a red

wines.tasting_notes_detailed: The flagship from old vines at Nemea's highest point. Ruby red with complex nose of black cherry, forest, leather, spice. Rich, delicious mouthfeel with opulent fruit and elegant spice. High altitude brings remarkable freshness and balance. If tasted blind, you'd think top-end Bordeaux or fine Rioja. Dense black fruit intensity, perfectly integrated oak, silky and mouth-filling, astonishingly well balanced.

critic_notes.virgin_wines: Tasted blind, you'd think very top-end Bordeaux. Dense, perfectly integrated oak, silky, mouth-filling, astonishingly balanced.
critic_notes.maltby_greek: Reminiscent of fine Rioja with richer fruit. High altitude brings welcome freshness.

wines.value_thesis: One of the best value wines in Europe. Organic, 40-year vines, 900m altitude, 12+ months oak, serious structure. €17 is extraordinary—equivalent quality Burgundy reds would be €60-80.

user_experience.reaction: This is the best Agiorgitiko I've ever had. 'This feels too cheap! A steal.'
user_experience.tasting_notes: Tasted like a high-end Rioja—vanilla, leathery notes but softer on the palate.
user_experience.significance: The value anchor. Proves world-class quality exists at €17 in Greece.
user_experience.thesis_fit: Core to the 3 SOMS value proposition: organic, 40-year old vines, highest altitude in Nemea, serious production, €17. This is the market inefficiency.
user_experience.also_tasted: 100% Agiorgitiko Rosé—I love Agiorgitiko rosé. It's slightly pungent, has character and richness for those wanting to enjoy a rosé with character.

wines.name: Symposium
wines.serving_note: Excellent served lightly chilled, Loire Cabernet Franc style

wineries.name: Dalamara
wineries.description: Sixth-generation family winery instrumental in helping Naoussa become Greece's first wine appellation in 1971. Located at the entrance of Naoussa town on the eastern foothills of Mount Vermio. Known for the 'Paliokalias' single vineyard—considered a Greek 'Grand Cru'. One of the finest Xinomavro producers in Greece.
wineries.philosophy: Organic since 1996, biodynamic (uncertified) since 2008. Self-sufficient—produces own fertilizer on the farm, 100% solar-powered winery. Minimal intervention: native yeasts, no added enzymes, no filtration, no clarification. Only intervention is small amount of sulfur.

owners.kostis_dalamara: Sixth generation. Studied oenology at Beaune, worked at Domaine Trapet (Gevrey-Chambertin), Clos du Rouge Gorge (Roussillon), plus Alsace and Catalonia. Took over from father in 2010.

vineyard_info.paliokalias_description: Greek 'Grand Cru'. Name means 'Old Watch Point'. Located 2.5 miles southeast of Naoussa town at 250-300m altitude. Deep groundwater, small river with stable water flow. South-facing slopes with full sun exposure.
vineyard_info.climate: Warm, dry summers; mild winters. Full sun exposure most of day.

production_style.oak_aging: 17 months in French oak (50% 300L, 50% 225L; 10% new, 90% 2nd-5th use)

wines.name: Paliokalias

tasting_profile.tannins: Powerful yet never intrusive, firm but not aggressive, deliciously dry

wines.tasting_notes_detailed: Astonishing power and concentration from the 'Grand Cru' of Naoussa. Pale in color but intensely aromatic with black tea, cherry, incense, bramble. Wonderfully concentrated palate with powerful yet never intrusive tannins. Like fine Barolo or aged Burgundy—Xinomavro shows dried red fruits, leather, game, spice, and tar. Needs time but rewards patience. One of the finest examples of Greek wine.

critic_scores.jancis_robinson: Serious wine that really needs time to show elegance. Silky complexity with age.
critic_scores.wine_society: May well be the best of the bunch. 2021 believed to be one of best vintages in Naoussa.

price.note: Third most expensive Xinomavro in Naoussa, price rising

aging_potential.note: Xinomavro is often compared to Nebbiolo—structured, complex, age-worthy

user_experience.connection: Met Kostis Dalamara through Vagelis at Lost Roots wine bar. Visited the winery.
user_experience.wines_tasted: All their wines. Tasted the 2022 vintage of Paliokalias which was not for sale yet.
user_experience.reaction: He's the OG of Xinomavro with Thymiopoulos. His wines rival anything from Burgundy, especially Paliokalias.
user_experience.personal_note: He's a great person too (see my Google reviews). Studied in France, speaks French.
user_experience.recommendation: His Naoussa is a great entry point for those looking to discover Xinomavro.
user_experience.significance: The entry point to serious Xinomavro. When Vagelis introduced you to this grape at Lost Roots, it started a journey through Naoussa.

wines.name: Naoussa Xinomavro
wines.description: The 'entry level' Dalamara—but don't let that fool you. Featured on wine lists at Noble Rot London. Fresh strawberries, sundried tomato, juicy cranberry, spice. Sensational and approachable in youth but with great aging capacity.
wines.note: Brilliant value, one of finest examples available

visit_info.experience: Cozy tasting room with roaring fire in winter. View from balcony over vineyards to Thessaloniki bay.
visit_info.nearby: Agios Nikolaos park, Vergina (Tomb of King Philip), Edessa springs

wineries.name: Thymiopoulos Vineyards
wineries.description: Multi-generational family of grape growers who began bottling their own wine in 2003. Apostolos Thymiopoulos has made Xinomavro one of the most prestigious wines on international wine lists. Most production exported abroad—recognized as one of the most interesting wineries in all of Greece.
wineries.philosophy: Certified organic and biodynamic farming. All wines fermented with wild yeast. Focus on expressing the elegance and complexity of Xinomavro without heavy-handed winemaking.

founder.name: Apostolos Thymiopoulos
founder.background: Family has grown grapes in Naoussa for generations. After studying oenology at University of Athens, returned to family vineyards to bottle under own label.
founder.achievement: The man who 'tamed Xinomavro'—made the grape accessible while preserving its serious character.

vineyard_info.soil_type: Limestone with schist, granite, and clay top-soil
vineyard_info.climate: Warm climate tempered by Mediterranean winds and air from Vermio mountain. High altitude and big diurnal range at Fytia adds complexity and finesse.

wines.name: Earth and Sky (Ghi kai Uranos)
wines.description: The biggest hit. Blend of best parcels from estate vineyards. Named 'Earth and Sky' (Ghi kai Uranos). One of the top 5 rated Naoussa wines by critics.
wines.tasting_notes_detailed: Exceptional—achieves rare combination of very full, concentrated fruit while maintaining lightness and high finesse. The sundried tomato of classic Naoussa is prominent but balanced by juicy red fruit. Fresh with robust tannins, intense fruit that will mellow and become remarkable over time. A wine you can fall into like falling into bed at the end of a long day.

critic_scores.note: One of top 5 Naoussa wines by critics

user_experience.reaction: One of the biggest steals. One of the other OGs alongside Dalamara.
user_experience.earth_sky_notes: These are cerebral wines, similar to Paliokalias.
user_experience.atma_white_notes: Genius. First time I heard of the concept of a blanc de noirs (white from red grapes).
user_experience.blanc_de_coteaux_notes: Exquisite and quite cerebral for minimal intervention wine lovers: fruity, soft on the palate and slightly buttery.
user_experience.significance: Wished wine lists had more producers like Thymiopoulos (mentioned at Marais bar in Thessaloniki). The man who tamed Xinomavro—makes it accessible while keeping the serious character.
user_experience.thesis_fit: Hard to find by the glass. Represents the quality of Naoussa Xinomavro that's under-recognized internationally.

wines.name: Young Vines Xinomavro
wines.description: Entry-level, unoaked. Fragrant and fresh expression with notes of red fruit and herbs. Underlying acidity and lingering finish.
wines.note: Great value introduction to Thymiopoulos style

wineries.name: Hatzidakis Winery
wineries.description: Legendary cave winery with cult status in the wine world. Santorini's only certified organic winery. Built literally beneath its own vineyard, carved into underground cave structures. Founded by visionary winemaker Haridimos Hatzidakis, who passed away suddenly in 2017. His pioneering work pushed Greek wine to the forefront of the international wine world.
wineries.philosophy: Organic and biodynamic methods in vineyards, minimal intervention in cellars. No herbicides, pesticides, or irrigation—humidity from sea breezes provides just enough moisture. Vines trained close to ground in unique bird's nest shape (kouloura). Focused on authentic expression of volcanic terroir.

legacy.haridimos_hatzidakis: Originally from Crete, previously winemaker at Boutari. Totally connected with the land. Being among his vines was what he loved. Created complex wines comparable to great wines of Jura, Loire, and Chablis. Passed away August 2017.
legacy.current_team: Now run by Stella Hatzidakis and Konstantina Chryssou (co-founder), continuing Haridimos's spirit and philosophy.

certification.organic_note: Only certified organic winery on Santorini

vineyard_info.location: Pyrgos Kallistis, on road to Profiti Ilias monastery—highest village in Santorini
vineyard_info.climate: Mediterranean—warm days, cool nights. Sea breezes provide humidity.

winery_info.style: Cave winery carved from volcanic tufa beneath vineyards
winery_info.design: Subterranean—natural temperature stability, bioclimatic building following traditional Santorini architecture
winery_info.philosophy: Did not uproot even one vine to build—precious grape quantities

wines.name: Santorini Familia
wines.description: Main wine of the estate—gold-standard Assyrtiko. Plots across the island owned by friends and family, hence 'Familia'. Powerful yet elegant, smoky, steely, sea-breezy, Chablis-esque charm.
wines.tasting_notes_detailed: Broad yet focused Assyrtiko—like a bodybuilder showing incredible weight and power whilst also being elegant and defined. Stone fruits, dried apricots and cashew on nose. Textured, long, saline palate. Gold-standard Assyrtiko with powerful yet elegant, smoky, steely, sea-breezy Chablis-esque charm.

user_experience.reaction: He specializes in 100% single-varietal bottles and is one of the OGs of Greek wines. All exquisite and quite cerebral.
user_experience.significance: Represents the pinnacle of Santorini organic winemaking. The visionary's legacy continues.

wines.name: Vinsanto
wines.description: Traditional Santorini sweet wine. An explosion of aromas: raisin, dried fruits, honey, caramel, coffee, chocolate, nuts, spices, mint, incense, minerality, graphite, gunpowder.
wines.tasting_notes_detailed: Endless aromatics. Raisin, dried fruits, honey, caramel, coffee, chocolate, nuts and spices alongside mint, incense, minerality, graphite and gunpowder. Complex, historic style.

user_experience.note: Capped dinner with Hatzidakis Vinsanto—referenced in your review of the winery experience.

wines.name: Mavrotragano
wines.description: Indigenous red grape of Santorini. Name roughly translates as 'black and crispy.' Complex, eccentric red wine made in the cave winery.
wines.note: Mavrotragano vineyard grows directly above the underground cave winery.

visit_info.location: Outside Pyrgos Kallisti, on road to Emporio
visit_info.experience: Underground cave beneath family vineyard. Relaxed, intimate atmosphere. Appointment only.

wineries.name: Ktima Gerovassiliou
wineries.description: Legendary estate that saved Malagousia from extinction. Vangelis Gerovassiliou is credited as the 'father of Malagousia.' Consistently ranked in Top 50 wineries worldwide. The benchmark for Greek aromatic whites.
wineries.philosophy: Single private vineyard cultivation with great care, producing wines that carry all characteristics of Epanomi terroir. Combination of tradition and modern technology.
wineries.historic_significance: Rescued Malagousia grape from extinction in 1981. First to vinify it commercially. Changed Greek wine history.

vineyard_info.location: Slopes of Epanomi, 25km south of Thessaloniki, surrounded by sea on 3 sides
vineyard_info.climate: Mediterranean with sea influence from Gulf

wines.name: Malagousia
wines.description: The benchmark Malagousia. Rescued grape variety, now planted throughout Greece, but Gerovasiliou's remains the standard by which all others are measured.

critic_scores.james_suckling_2022: 93 - Floral jasmine to roses and chamomile, melon and lychee. Silky and oily, full body, fantastic freshness, salty mineral.
critic_scores.note: Gold Medal Mundus Vini, Gold Medal Berliner Wein Trophy

visit_info.note: Stunning modern architecture with outdoor art

user_experience.reaction: The O.G. of Malagousia. He brought the grape back from the brink.
user_experience.tasting_notes: The fruit forwardness on his Malagousia is insane! Citrusy to me, melony. It's a cacophony of flavors in the mouth.
user_experience.significance: The benchmark. Without Gerovasiliou, this grape would be extinct.

wineries.name: Ktima Ligas (Domaine Ligas)
wineries.description: Pioneer of organic farming and natural wine in Greece. Family winery now run by Meli (Melissanthi) Ligas. Known for zero-sulfur wines, permaculture practices, and reviving rare indigenous varieties. Solar-powered since 2003.
wineries.philosophy: Low-intervention, zero-sulfur philosophy. Permaculture in vineyards. Spontaneous fermentation with indigenous yeasts. No fining, no filtration. 'Wine is not the sole product of a vineyard—there are other plants and species that rely on that land too.'

founders.thomas_ligas: Studied oenology in France. Pioneer of organic farming in Greece. 'My father always wanted to work with Roditis on its own.'

wines.name: Maison Viticole Ligas Assyrtiko

production_details.skin_contact: Several days (somewhere between white and orange)
production_details.fermentation: Spontaneous in stainless steel and old oak

tasting_profile.texture: Touch of tannin from skin contact, super clean, very drinkable

wines.note: Works for many moments—brunch, dinner, late night drinks

wines.name: Lamda Assyrtiko
wines.note: Grown on 2-meter pergolas to emphasize mineral character

user_experience.context: Had a minimal intervention Assyrtiko from him with a full course fish meal at Murga in Thessaloniki. The whole bottle accompanied the meal perfectly.
user_experience.tasting_notes: The expression of the Assyrtiko, similar to the Julia Assyrtiko, speaks of the Northern Greece terroir to me: it's less mineral than on Santorini, a bit more fruity and pungent (this perhaps thanks to Ligas' natural winemaking practices).
user_experience.insight: Northern Greece Assyrtiko = different terroir expression. Less volcanic minerality, more fruit-forward.

wineries.name: Monemvasia Winery (Tsimbidi)
wineries.description: Family winery dedicated to reviving the historic Malvasia wine and rescuing almost-forgotten grapes like Kydonitsa and Monemvasia. Resurrected PDO Monemvasia-Malvasia sweet wine (first modern bottling 2013). Physicist-turned-winemaker George Tsimbidis founded it as a passion project.
wineries.philosophy: Investing solely in Greek local varieties. Positioning Monemvasia's terroir on the global wine map. Modern winemaking applied to indigenous heritage grapes.

founders.george_tsimbidis: Physicist by profession, wine lover by passion. Native of Velies village. Love for history and homeland drove the resurrection of Malvasia.
founders.second_generation: Daughters Marialena, Anastasia (winemaking), Artemis (in enological studies)

wineries.the_malvasia_project: Historical research, vineyard selection, winemaking to resurrect the medieval sweet wine that once graced sophisticated Mediterranean tables. PDO recognition achieved 2010, first modern bottling 2013.

wines.name: Kydonitsa
wines.description: Flagship white from rare indigenous variety. Crown jewel of the winery. Nearly extinct grape revived.
food_pairings: White meat with lemon and herbs

wines.name: Monemvasia (Dry)
wines.description: Dry version of the historic grape. Citrus, stone fruit, wildflower notes.

wines.name: Assyrtiko
wines.note: Tsimbidis believe Assyrtiko was likely part of the historical grape mix here, increasingly important in warming climate

wines.name: Monemvasia-Malvasia
wines.production: Sun-dried grapes (8-15 days), concentrated sugars, slow fermentation, oak aging
wines.historic_significance: First modern bottling of this medieval wine since Ottoman occupation

user_experience.reaction: The king and queen of Laconia, part of the ancient Spartan heartland. Family winery.
user_experience.kydonitsa_notes: A rare and interesting grape. Mineral, Assyrtiko-like but more accessible, a bit more capricious. It's an accessible, affordable cerebral wine.
user_experience.monemvasia_notes: Sharing a name with the town nearby, comes from the name of a wine that was prized all over the area in Byzantine times, and beyond the Byzantine world. Smooth and silky red. Not as acidic as Xinomavro, neither vanilla-y nor leathery as Agiorgitiko.
user_experience.assyrtiko_notes: Quite interesting: not as mineral as on Santorini, but still very buttery and smooth, with a beautiful golden color.
user_experience.thesis_fit: Historic grapes from historic land. Byzantine wine heritage revived.

wineries.name: Ieropoulos Family Winery
wineries.description: Boutique winery founded by Chronis Ieropoulos, applying traditional techniques learned from his grandfather in Kastoria mountains to the Nemea terroir. Focus on expressing history and tradition of Nemea through modern methods.
wineries.philosophy: Grandfather's principles combined with advanced technology. Minimal intervention winemaking using only free-run juice. Vineyards as the absolute epicenter.

founder.chronis_ieropoulos: Learned wine secrets from grandfather in Kastoria mountains. Built model winery in Nemea in 2008.

vineyard_info.winery_architecture: Dark stones excavated from neighboring mountains, blending into landscape

wines.name: Saint George (Rosé)
wines.description: Elegant rosé embodying the versatility of Agiorgitiko. Aromatic, gentle, refreshing acidity.

wines.name: Chrysovoulon (Daemon)
wines.description: Premium red—'Nemea meets Burgundy.' Combines finesse with aging ability.

user_experience.tasting_notes: Lighter and similar in taste to the Rosé from Bizios of the same grape.

wineries.name: Domaine Costa Lazaridi (Château Julia)
wineries.description: One of Greece's most celebrated family wineries. First to plant Assyrtiko in Drama. Over 200 hectares—one of the biggest private linear vineyards in Greece. Certified Regenerative by A Greener World.
wineries.philosophy: Regenerative viticulture. 'Winemakers who work in harmony with the land, crafting wines that respect and reflect the place they come from.'

current_generation.george_lazaridis: Winemaker, oversees viticulture, leads regenerative initiative

certification.regenerative: Certified Regenerative by A Greener World (AGW)

vineyard_info.location: Slopes of Adriani, Drama, bordered by Natura 2000 zones

wines.name: Château Julia Assyrtiko
wines.description: First Assyrtiko planted in Drama by Costa Lazaridi—now integral to region's identity. Aegean personality with international character.

tasting_profile.color: Bright pale yellow with green hues

critic_scores.note: Multiple Gold medals: Mundus Vini, Berliner Wein Trophy, Concours Mondial

wines.name: Château Julia Agiorgitiko
wines.description: Twenty years of cultivation in Drama terroir with modern practices.

production_details.vessels: French oak 500L, oak and concrete tanks

user_experience.context: Unlike many of the other wineries, this is a rather large and commercial producer.
user_experience.reaction: Still, their Agiorgitiko rivals any high-end Rioja at a fraction of the price.
user_experience.assyrtiko_notes: Similar expression to Ligas—speaks of Northern Greece terroir. Less mineral than Santorini, more fruity and pungent.
user_experience.thesis_fit: Proves even larger producers in Greece deliver world-class value.

wineries.name: Tetramythos Winery
wineries.description: Pioneer of natural winemaking in Greece. High-altitude organic vineyards (up to 1050m) on slopes of Mount Helmos/Aroania overlooking Gulf of Corinth. Known for reviving Mavro Kalavrytino from near extinction.
wineries.philosophy: Minimal intervention, sense of place. Natural wines ('Natur' range) with spontaneous fermentation, no added sulfites, unfined, unfiltered. Harvested on flower days (whites) and fruit days (reds) per biodynamic calendar.

founders.panagiotis_papagiannopoulos: Winemaker—talented, highly inspired, native of the area

vineyard_info.climate: Mediterranean mitigated by Gulf of Corinth breezes. Among coolest mesoclimates in Greece.

production_style.sulfur: Minimal or zero in Natur range

wines.name: Volitsa Natur
wines.description: Ultra-rare indigenous grape found via a local shepherd. One of the most unexplored Greek varieties, found only in highland Peloponnese vineyards.

critic_scores.wineanorak: 95 points - 'Sappy with great energy. Light and peppery with some green hints. Such freshness and focus with cherry and raspberry. White pepper and plum. So lovely.'

user_experience.reaction: A rare grape, a light summer red that rivals some Burgundy.
user_experience.tasting_notes: Lighter than a Pinot, but with character, acidity and fruit forwardness that screams terroir. It's something you'll directly recognize as unique when you taste it.
user_experience.thesis_fit: Perfect example of 3 SOMS thesis: unknown grape, terroir-driven, rivals Burgundy at fraction of price.

wines.name: Mavro Kalavrytino
wines.description: The only monovarietal wine from this rare variety. Grape single-handedly saved from extinction by Tetramythos. Name means 'Black of Kalavryta.'

critic_scores.decanter_2022: 90 points - Burgundian touch, tobacco, cocoa, lingering spiciness

wines.name: Malagousia
wines.note: Featured on Noble Rot London wine lists

wines.name: Retsina Natur (Amphora)
wines.description: Made the traditional way—in Cretan amphorae, as done for thousands of years. One of the very few Retsinas that changes what you think Retsina can be.
wines.note: Featured on Noble Rot wine lists

visit_info.location: Ano Diakopto village, 5km from Diakofto on road to Kalavryta ski resort

user_experience.reaction: Pioneer of natural winemaking in Greece. The Volitsa is the standout.
user_experience.volitsa_discovery: A rare grape, a light summer red that rivals some Burgundy: lighter than a Pinot, but with character, acidity and fruit forwardness that screams terroir. Something you'll directly recognize as unique when you taste it.
user_experience.thesis_fit: Rare indigenous grapes saved from extinction, high altitude, natural winemaking, world-class results at Greek prices.

wineries.name: Santo Wines
wineries.description: Santorini's largest producer and only cooperative winery, representing 1,200+ grape farmers across the island. Founded as 'Santorini Vine & Wine Protection Fund' in 1911, reorganized 1947. State-of-the-art winery built 1992 on caldera rim with legendary sunset views.
wineries.philosophy: Authentic Santorini wines of superior quality while respecting traditional vinification techniques and protecting the precious vineyards. Cooperative model supports island's farming families.

wines.name: Santorini Assyrtiko
wines.note: Entry-level Santorini Assyrtiko—'Assyrtiko on training wheels' but solid introduction to the grape

visit_info.experience: Stunning caldera views, sunset tastings, restaurant with food pairings
visit_info.recognition: Wine Enthusiast: Best place in Santorini to taste wine
visit_info.note: Most visited winery on island—come for view and introduction, visit boutique producers (Hatzidakis, Sigalas) for depth

wineries.name: Domaine Tselepos
wineries.description: Pioneer of the Greek Wine Renaissance. Yiannis Tselepos rescued Moschofilero from oblivion by vinifying it as a mono-varietal, highlighting its unique characteristics. Studied oenology at University of Dijon in Burgundy. Also produces Agiorgitiko (Nemea) and Assyrtiko (Santorini).
wineries.philosophy: 'A grape does not come to you, you must go to it.' Holistic approach to winemaking—small chapel to Saint Tryphon (patron saint of winemakers) sits at highest point of vineyard.

founder.yiannis_tselepos: Born 1955 in Paphos, Cyprus. Studied oenology at Dijon. Moved to Arcadia 1981. Founded winery with wife Amalia 1989.

vineyard_info.climate: Continental—late ripening, preserves acidity and aroma

wines.name: Mantinia
wines.description: Flagship wine. Blanc de gris (white from pink/grey grapes). The variety Tselepos rescued from oblivion.

critic_scores.note: Top 5 Mantinia wines. Gold/Double Gold at Balkan International Wine Competition

wines.name: Amalia Brut
wines.description: Named after wife Amalia. First traditional method sparkling from Moschofilero grapes in Greece.

wineries.recognition: Pioneer of Greek Wine Renaissance. Closely associated with Moschofilero's rise to prominence.

wineries.name: Ktima Papargiriou
wineries.description: Small family estate known for elegant, terroir-driven wines. Focus on indigenous varieties with minimal intervention approach.
wineries.note: Part of founder's tasting journey—mentioned in context document

wineries.name: Roussos Winery
wineries.description: Historic Santorini producer. Traditional approach to volcanic wines.
wineries.note: Part of founder's Santorini tasting exploration

wineries.name: Grampas Winery
wineries.description: Specialist in Verdea—traditional oxidative wine style unique to Zakynthos. Historic style dating back centuries.

wines.name: Verdea
wines.description: Traditional Zakynthian style. Oxidative, amber-colored, complex. Made from local varieties using ancestral methods.

wineries.note: Unique regional style—Verdea is to Zakynthos what Vinsanto is to Santorini

what_these_wines_prove: 150-year-old vines on volcanic terroir (Papas) = transcendent wine for €100 vs €150-240 Burgundy equivalent
First single-vineyard Assyrtiko (Kavalieros) = pioneering terroir expression for €100
Rescued extinct grape (Mouhtaro) = unique character nobody outside Greece knows
Organic, high-altitude, old-vine reds (Bizios) = world-class quality at €17
thesis_summary.market_inefficiency: Greek wines of world-class quality selling at 50-70% cheaper than equivalent Burgundy, Napa, or Tuscany. Not because they're inferior—because Greece hasn't entered global fine wine consciousness yet.
thesis_summary.timing: Pre-discovery. 5 years before mainstream market awakening. Prices will move 2-3x.
